title: Writing Files ✍️
description: Create and write data to files.

slides.title: Writing to Files ✍️
slides.content:
# Creating & Writing Files

Use `"w"` mode to **write** (creates or overwrites) and `"a"` to **append**:

```
# Write mode — creates/overwrites
with open("output.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write("Hello World\n")
    f.write("Second line\n")

# Append mode — adds to end
with open("output.txt", "a") as f:
    f.write("Appended line\n")
```

⚠️ **Warning:** `"w"` mode **erases everything** in the file first! Use `"a"` to keep existing content.

slides.title: Write vs Append 🎬
slides.content: See the difference between write and append:
slides.codeSnippet:
# Write (creates fresh)
with open("notes.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write("Note 1: Python is great\n")
    f.write("Note 2: Practice daily\n")

# Append (adds to end)
with open("notes.txt", "a") as f:
    f.write("Note 3: Stay curious\n")

# Read everything
with open("notes.txt", "r") as f:
    print(f.read())

quizData.question: What does "w" mode do to an existing file?
quizData.explanation: "w" mode erases the entire file before writing. Use "a" to keep existing content.

quizData.question: Which mode adds content to the end of an existing file?
quizData.explanation: "a" (append) mode adds content to the end without erasing existing data.
